frameworks.name: evidence integration
frameworks.one_line: decisions combine multiple pieces of evidence to discriminate between alternatives

frameworks.name: normative probabilistic inference
frameworks.one_line: integration as Bayesian-style accumulation extended from milliseconds to tens of seconds

frameworks.name: neural network models of integration
frameworks.one_line: network dynamics impose acute timescale limits that this work challenges

core_question: Can evidence integration models that explain rapid perceptual decisions generalize to deliberations unfolding over tens of seconds?
key_assumptions: evidence integration is the canonical mechanism for perceptual decision-making
normative integration combines independent samples probabilistically
memory leak and noise can be quantified across long inter-sample gaps
manipulations: duration and unpredictability of gaps between visual evidence samples
number and informativeness of brief evidence samples
extended timescale of deliberation up to tens of seconds
key_findings: humans integrate brief visual samples separated by long unpredictable gaps
behavior approximates a normative integration process across tens of seconds
no significant memory leak or accumulation noise emerges over prolonged timescales
interpretations: evidence integration generalizes beyond rapid judgments to extended deliberation
computational insights from short-timescale tasks extend to naturalistic long decisions
